or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')

Specific human voice vs committee 4/10 — Reads like editorial SEO copy with occasional attempts at personality; no consistent narrator.

patch.target: This itinerary balances non-negotiable Oahu sights, real downtime, and enough food stops to placate even the pickiest friend.
patch.before: This itinerary balances non-negotiable Oahu sights, real downtime, and enough food stops to placate even the pickiest friend.
patch.after: I built this around what my own group actually did—beaches in the morning, shrimp trucks at noon, and nobody yelling about dinner plans.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 4/10: Reads like editorial SEO copy with occasional attempts at personality; no consistent narrator.

instructions.evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Has some opinions (Ala Moana beats hotels, skip the spreadsheet) but most are hedged or generic travel-blog takes.
Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — Mix of fresh phrases ('friend-proof', 'group meltdowns') and tired ones ('wishful thinking', 'hard-won advice').

patch.target: Coordinating friends in Oahu requires more than wishful thinking, it needs a plan with real places and actual decision points, or you'll end up missing sunset at Pipeline, again.
patch.before: Coordinating friends in Oahu requires more than wishful thinking, it needs a plan with real places and actual decision points, or you'll end up missing sunset at Pipeline, again.
patch.after: Coordinating friends on Oahu is mostly damage control: book the reservations or you'll be eating gas station musubi while Pipeline glows pink without you.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: Mix of fresh phrases ('friend-proof', 'group meltdowns') and tired ones ('wishful thinking', 'hard-won advice').

instructions.evidence: Point of view vs both-sides 6/10 — Has a POV on groups but the comparison sections and FAQ often list both sides neutrally.

patch.target: Oahu is set up for both, beaches, hikes, and museums are family-proof, but most friend groups will want to set expectations for late dinners and city nightlife if traveling with kids.
patch.before:
t;text": "Oahu is set up for both, beaches, hikes, and museums are family-proof, but most friend groups will want to set expectations for late dinners and city nightlife if traveling with kids."
          }
patch.after:
t;text": "Oahu skews family-friendly by default; if your friend group wants late dinners and Chinatown bars, leave the kids with grandma."
          }
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs both-sides" scored 6/10: Has a POV on groups but the comparison sections and FAQ often list both sides neutrally. (before extended with context for unique match)

instructions.evidence: Quotable sentences 4/10 — A few near-quotable lines but most are functional; nothing a friend would screenshot.

patch.target: Friend groups don't need more platitudes, you need dates, places, and backup backups.
patch.before: Friend groups don't need more platitudes, you need dates, places, and backup backups.
patch.after: Friend groups don't die from bad weather—they die in the group chat, somewhere between 'whenever works' and 'I'm flexible.'
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentences" scored 4/10: A few near-quotable lines but most are functional; nothing a friend would screenshot.

instructions.evidence: Intro earns attention 5/10 — Opens with a header dump and a generic stat before getting to the sharper 'one organiser, seven opinions' line.

patch.target: Waikiki attracts over a million visitors each year, and in 2026, group trip planning is no less chaotic than it was five years ago.
patch.before: Waikiki attracts over a million visitors each year, and in 2026, group trip planning is no less chaotic than it was five years ago.
patch.after: There's a specific moment on every group trip when someone suggests Hanauma Bay and you realize nobody booked it. This guide prevents that moment.
patch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 5/10: Opens with a header dump and a generic stat before getting to the sharper 'one organiser, seven opinions' line.
